description: Pre-stuffed failed write — does the model adapt (mkdir + retry) or give up or loop?

messages.content: You are a coding assistant. Use tools to complete tasks. When a tool returns an error, read the error message and adapt your approach.
Create the file src/utils/helpers.py with the content 'def noop(): pass'.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

expect.notes: Optimal: bash(cmd='mkdir -p src/utils') or bash+write. Failure modes: (a) gives up with text response, (b) retries write identically, (c) calls non-existent mkdir tool.
